steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is C, $\sqrt{35z}$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Rewrite the half-power as a square root
steps.content: A rational exponent of $\tfrac{1}{2}$ is the principal square root: for any $a\ge 0$, $a^{1/2}=\sqrt{a}$. Applying this with $a=35z$:$$(35z)^{1/2}=\sqrt{35z}.$$

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• A: $35\sqrt{z}$ would equal $\sqrt{35^2\cdot z}=\sqrt{1225z}$, not $\sqrt{35z}$.
  • B: $\sqrt{35}\cdot z$ equals $\sqrt{35\cdot z^2}=\sqrt{35z^2}$, not $\sqrt{35z}$.
  • C: $\sqrt{35z}$ — identical to the expression we derived.
  • D: $\sqrt{(35z)^2}=|35z|=35z$ (since $z\gt 1$), which is the original expression squared, not its square root.
That matches Choice C — $\sqrt{35z}$.
